title: Evolutionary transfer learning enables organism-wide inference of mammalian enhancer landscapes
abstract: STEAM (Synteny-aware Transfer learning for Enhancer Activity Modeling) expands training corpus to include enhancer orthologs from up to 241 mammalian genomes (Zoonomia). Profiled 3.9M nuclei from 36 staged mouse embryos (E10-P0), resolving 36 cell classes and 140 cell types. Predicts enhancers for all major developmental lineages across 241 mammalian genomes (7,712 genome-wide enhancer tracks). Addresses overprediction at tandem repeats and conflation of promoter/distal enhancer grammars.
